name: nshrunner
documentation:
# nshrunner

nshrunner is a Python library that provides a unified way to run functions in various environments, such as local dev machines, cloud VMs, SLURM clusters, and LSF clusters. It was created to simplify the process of running ML training jobs across multiple machines and environments.

## Motivation

When running ML training jobs on different machines and environments, it can be challenging to manage the specifics of each environment. nshrunner was developed to address this issue by providing a single function that can be used to run jobs on any supported environment without having to worry about the details of each environment.

## Features

- Supports running functions locally, on SLURM clusters, and on LSF clusters
- Provides a unified interface for running functions across different environments
- Allows for easy configuration of job options, such as resource requirements and environment variables
- Supports snapshotting the environment to ensure reproducibility, using the [`nshsnap`](https://www.github.com/nimashoghi/nshsnap) library
- Provides utilities for logging, seeding, and signal handling

## Installation

nshrunner can be installed using pip:

```bash
pip install nshrunner
```

## Usage

Here's a simple example of how to use nshrunner to run a function locally:

```python
import nshrunner as R

def run_fn(x: int):
    return x + 5

runs = [(1,)]

runner = R.Runner(run_fn, R.RunnerConfig(working_dir="."))
list(runner.local(runs))
```

To run the same function on a SLURM cluster:

```python
runner.submit_slurm(
    runs,
    {
        "partition": "learnaccel",
        "nodes": 4,
        "ntasks_per_node": 8,  # Change this to limit # of GPUs
        "gpus_per_task": 1,
        "cpus_per_task": 1,
    },
    snapshot=True,
)
```

And on an LSF cluster:

```python
runner.submit_lsf(
    runs,
    {
        "summit": True,
        "queue": "learnaccel",
        "nodes": 4,
        "rs_per_node": 8,  # Change this to limit # of GPUs
    },
    snapshot=True,
)
```

For more detailed usage examples, please refer to the documentation.

## Acknowledgements

`nshrunner` is heavily inspired by [`submitit`](https://github.com/facebookincubator/submitit). It builds on `submitit`'s design and adds support for LSF clusters, snapshotting, and other features.

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! For feature requests, bug reports, or questions, please open an issue on GitHub. If you'd like to contribute code, please submit a pull request with your changes.

## License

nshrunner is released under the MIT License. See `LICENSE` for more information.

functions.name: nshrunner._submit._util._write_run_metadata_commands
documentation:
Creates a list of bash commands that will write the run metadata
to the submission directory. For the parent script:
- We want to save the job id (in plain text) to a file called `job_id.txt`
- We want to save the environment variables to a file called `env.txt`
- We'll also create a run.json file
For worker scripts:
- We create the same files, but in a /meta/workers/{rank}/ directory

functions.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.to_array_batch_script
documentation: Create the batch script for the job.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.name
documentation:
The name of the job.

This corresponds to the "-J" option in bsub.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.queue
documentation:
The queue to submit the job to.

This corresponds to the "-q" option in bsub. If not specified, the default queue will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.output_file
documentation:
The file to write the job output to.

This corresponds to the "-o" option in bsub. If not specified, the output will be written to the default output file.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.error_file
documentation:
The file to write the job errors to.

This corresponds to the "-e" option in bsub. If not specified, the errors will be written to the default error file.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.walltime
documentation:
The maximum walltime for the job.

This corresponds to the "-W" option in bsub. The format is "HH:MM" or "MM". If not specified, the default walltime will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.memory_mb
documentation:
The maximum memory for the job in MB.

This corresponds to the "-M" option in bsub. If not specified, the default memory limit will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.cpu_limit
documentation:
The CPU time limit for the job in minutes.

This corresponds to the "-c" option in bsub. If not specified, the default CPU limit will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.rerunnable
documentation:
Whether the job should be rerunnable.

This corresponds to the "-r" option in bsub. If set to True, the job will be rerun if it fails due to a system failure.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.dependency_conditions
documentation:
The dependency conditions for the job.

This corresponds to the "-w" option in bsub. Each condition is a string that specifies the dependency condition.
Multiple conditions can be specified, and they will be combined using logical AND.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.email
documentation:
The email address to send notifications to.

This corresponds to the "-u" option in bsub. If specified, job notifications will be sent to this email address.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.notify_begin
documentation:
Whether to send a notification when the job begins.

This corresponds to the "-B" option in bsub. If set to True, a notification will be sent when the job begins.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.notify_end
documentation:
Whether to send a notification when the job ends.

This corresponds to the "-N" option in bsub. If set to True, a notification will be sent when the job ends.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.setup_commands
documentation:
The setup commands to run before the job.

These commands will be executed prior to everything else in the job script.
They will be included in both the worker script (the one that runs inside of srun/jsrun)
and not the submission script (the one that is submitted to the job scheduler).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.submission_script_setup_commands
documentation:
Same as `setup_commands`, but only for the submission script (and not
the worker scripts executed by srun/jsrun).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.environment
documentation:
The environment variables to set for the job.

These variables will be set prior to executing any commands in the job script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.project
documentation:
The project to charge the job to.

This corresponds to the "-P" option in bsub. If specified, the job will be charged to this project.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.nodes
documentation:
The number of nodes to use for the job.

This corresponds to the "-nnodes" option in bsub. The default is 1 node.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.rs_per_node
documentation: The number of resource sets per node. This is sent to the `jsrun` command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.cpus_per_rs
documentation: The number of CPUs per resource set. This is sent to the `jsrun` command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.gpus_per_rs
documentation: The number of GPUs per resource set. This is sent to the `jsrun` command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.tasks_per_rs
documentation: The number of tasks per resource set. This is sent to the `jsrun` command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.alloc_flags
documentation:
The allocation flags for the job.

This corresponds to the "-alloc_flags" option in bsub. If specified, the job will be allocated using these flags.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.command_prefix
documentation:
A command to prefix the job command with.

This is used to add commands like `jsrun` to the job command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.timeout_signal
documentation:
The signal to send to the job as the "warning action".

This corresponds to the "-wa" option in bsub.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.preempt_signal
documentation: The signal to send to the job when it is preempted.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.timeout_signal_time
documentation:
The time (before the job ends) to send the signal.

This corresponds to the "-wt" option in bsub.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.login_shell
documentation:
The login shell to use for the job.

This corresponds to the "-L" option in bsub. If specified, the job will be run using this login shell.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.on_exit_script_support
documentation:
Whether to support running an on-exit script outside of jsrun.

This is done by setting the environment variable `NSHRUNNER_EXIT_SCRIPT_DIR` to the path of an initially empty directory.
Whenever the script wants something to be done on exit, it should write a bash script to this directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.emit_metadata
documentation:
Whether to emit metadata about the job submission.

If True (default), the following metadata will be written to a JSON file:
- When you submit the job, the job submission information (command, script path, number of jobs, config, and environment) will be written to a file named `submission.json` in the `meta` directory.
- When the job starts running, the job ID and environment variables will be written to a file named `run.json` in the `meta` directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs.summit
documentation:
Whether the job is being submitted to Summit.

If set to True, the job will be submitted to Summit and the default Summit options will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.lsf.LSFJobKwargs._exit_script_dir
documentation: The directory to write the on-exit scripts to. (Internal use only)

functions.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.to_array_batch_script
documentation: Create the batch script for the job.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.name
documentation: The name of the job.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.screen_log_file
documentation: The path to the log file to write the output to. This is done by setting the `-L` flag to `screen`.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.output_file
documentation: The file to write the job output to.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.error_file
documentation: The file to write the job errors to.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.setup_commands
documentation:
The setup commands to run before the job.

These commands will be executed prior to everything else in the job script.
They will be included in both the worker script (the one that runs inside of srun/jsrun)
and not the submission script (the one that is submitted to the job scheduler).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.submission_script_setup_commands
documentation:
Same as `setup_commands`, but only for the submission script (and not
the worker scripts executed by srun/jsrun).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.environment
documentation:
The environment variables to set for the job.

These variables will be set prior to executing any commands in the job script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.command_prefix
documentation:
A command to prefix the job command with.

This is used to add commands like `srun` to the job command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.attach
documentation: Whether to attach to the screen session after starting it.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.pause_before_exit
documentation: Whether to pause before exiting the screen session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.emit_metadata
documentation:
Whether to emit metadata about the job submission.

If True (default), the following metadata will be written to a JSON file:
- When you submit the job, the job submission information (command, script path, number of jobs, config, and environment) will be written to a file named `submission.json` in the `meta` directory.
- When the job starts running, the job ID and environment variables will be written to a file named `run.json` in the `meta` directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs.on_exit_script_support
documentation:
Whether to support running an on-exit script outside of srun.

This is done by setting the environment variable `NSHRUNNER_EXIT_SCRIPT_DIR` to the path of an initially empty directory.
Whenever the script wants something to be done on exit, it should write a bash script to this directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.screen.ScreenJobKwargs._exit_script_dir
documentation: The directory to write the on-exit scripts to. (Internal use only)

functions.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm._determine_gres
documentation:
There are many different ways to specify GPU resources, but some are buggy.

This function normalizes all other ways to specify GPU resources to the `gres` option.

functions.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.to_array_batch_script
documentation: Create the batch script for the job.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.name
documentation:
The name of the job.

This corresponds to the "-J" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.account
documentation:
The account to charge resources used by this job to.

This corresponds to the "-A" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.partition
documentation:
The partition to submit the job to.

This corresponds to the "-p" option in sbatch. If not specified, the default partition will be used.
Multiple partitions can be specified, and they will be combined using logical OR.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.qos
documentation:
The quality of service to submit the job to.

This corresponds to the "--qos" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.output_file
documentation:
The file to write the job output to.

This corresponds to the "-o" option in sbatch. If not specified, the output will be written to the default output file.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.error_file
documentation:
The file to write the job errors to.

This corresponds to the "-e" option in sbatch. If not specified, the errors will be written to the default error file.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.time
documentation:
The maximum time for the job.

This corresponds to the "-t" option in sbatch. A value of 0 means no time limit.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.memory_mb
documentation:
The maximum memory for the job in MB.

This corresponds to the "--mem" option in sbatch. If not specified, the default memory limit will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.memory_per_cpu_mb
documentation:
The minimum memory required per usable allocated CPU.

This corresponds to the "--mem-per-cpu" option in sbatch. If not specified, the default memory limit will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.memory_per_gpu_mb
documentation:
The minimum memory required per allocated GPU.

This corresponds to the "--mem-per-gpu" option in sbatch. If not specified, the default memory limit will be used.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.cpus_per_task
documentation:
Advise the Slurm controller that ensuing job steps will require _ncpus_ number of processors per task.

This corresponds to the "-c" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.nodes
documentation:
The number of nodes to use for the job.

This corresponds to the "-N" option in sbatch. The default is 1 node.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.ntasks
documentation:
The number of tasks to use for the job.

This corresponds to the "-n" option in sbatch. The default is 1 task.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.ntasks_per_node
documentation:
The number of tasks for each node.

This corresponds to the "--ntasks-per-node" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.constraint
documentation:
Nodes can have features assigned to them by the Slurm administrator. Users can specify which of these features are required by their job using the constraint option.

This corresponds to the "-C" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.gres
documentation:
Specifies a comma-delimited list of generic consumable resources.

This corresponds to the "--gres" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.gpus
documentation:
Specify the total number of GPUs required for the job. An optional GPU type specification can be supplied.

This corresponds to the "-G" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.gpus_per_node
documentation:
Specify the number of GPUs required for the job on each node included in the job's resource allocation. An optional GPU type specification can be supplied.

This corresponds to the "--gpus-per-node" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.gpus_per_task
documentation:
Specify the number of GPUs required for the job on each task to be spawned in the job's resource allocation. An optional GPU type specification can be supplied.

This corresponds to the "--gpus-per-task" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.mail_user
documentation:
User to receive email notification of state changes as defined by mail_type.

This corresponds to the "--mail-user" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.mail_type
documentation:
Notify user by email when certain event types occur.

This corresponds to the "--mail-type" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.dependency
documentation:
Defer the start of this job until the specified dependencies have been satisfied.

This corresponds to the "-d" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.exclusive
documentation:
The job allocation can not share nodes with other running jobs.

This corresponds to the "--exclusive" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.timeout_signal
documentation:
The signal to send to the job when the job is being terminated.

This corresponds to the "--signal" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.timeout_signal_delay
documentation:
The delay before sending the signal to the job.

This corresponds to the "--signal ...@[delay]" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.preempt_signal
documentation: The signal to send to the job when it is preempted.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.open_mode
documentation:
The open mode for the output and error files.

This corresponds to the "--open-mode" option in sbatch.

Valid values are "append" and "truncate".

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.requeue
documentation:
Requeues the job if it's pre-empted.

This corresponds to the "--requeue" option in sbatch.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.setup_commands
documentation:
The setup commands to run before the job.

These commands will be executed prior to everything else in the job script.
They will be included in both the worker script (the one that runs inside of srun/jsrun)
and not the submission script (the one that is submitted to the job scheduler).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.submission_script_setup_commands
documentation:
Same as `setup_commands`, but only for the submission script (and not
the worker scripts executed by srun/jsrun).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.environment
documentation:
The environment variables to set for the job.

These variables will be set prior to executing any commands in the job script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.command_prefix
documentation:
A command to prefix the job command with.

This is used to add commands like `srun` to the job command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.srun_flags
documentation: The flags to pass to the `srun` command.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.emit_metadata
documentation:
Whether to emit metadata about the job submission.

If True (default), the following metadata will be written to a JSON file:
- When you submit the job, the job submission information (command, script path, number of jobs, config, and environment) will be written to a file named `submission.json` in the `meta` directory.
- When the job starts running, the job ID and environment variables will be written to a file named `run.json` in the `meta` directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs.on_exit_script_support
documentation:
Whether to support running an on-exit script outside of srun.

This is done by setting the environment variable `NSHRUNNER_EXIT_SCRIPT_DIR` to the path of an initially empty directory.
Whenever the script wants something to be done on exit, it should write a bash script to this directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._submit.slurm.SlurmJobKwargs._exit_script_dir
documentation: The directory to write the on-exit scripts to. (Internal use only)

classes.name: nshrunner.session.Session

methods.name: nshrunner.session.Session.from_current_session
documentation:
Create a Session instance from the current environment variables.
Returns None if not in a Runner session.

methods.name: nshrunner.session.Session.write_exit_script
documentation: Write an exit script to the session directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.session_id
documentation: Unique identifier for the current session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.session_dir
documentation: Directory path for the current session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.snapshot_dir
documentation: Directory path for the snapshot, if available.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.snapshot_modules
documentation: List of snapshot modules, if available.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.is_worker_script
documentation: Indicates if this is running as a worker script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_base_dir
documentation: Base directory for job submission, if applicable.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_job_index
documentation: Index of the current job in a job array, if applicable.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_timeout_signal
documentation: Signal number to be used for job timeout, if specified.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_preempt_signal
documentation: Signal number to be used for job preemption, if specified.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_local_rank
documentation: Local rank of the current process, if applicable.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_global_rank
documentation: Global rank of the current process, if applicable.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_world_size
documentation: Total number of processes in the job, if applicable.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.exit_script_dir
documentation: Directory for exit scripts, if specified.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.session.Session.submit_interface_module
documentation: Name of the submit interface module, if specified.
Represents the current Runner execution session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner._Session.id
documentation: The ID of the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner._Session.dir_path
documentation: The path to the session directory.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner._Session.env
documentation: Environment variables to set for the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner._Session.snapshot
documentation: The snapshot information for the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.working_dir
documentation:
The `working_dir` parameter is a string that represents the directory where the program will save its execution files and logs.
    This is used when submitting the program to a SLURM/LSF cluster or when using the `local_sessions` method.
    If `None`, this will default to the current working directory / `nshrunner`.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.python_logging
documentation: Logging configuration for the runner.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.seed
documentation: Seed configuration for the runner.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.env
documentation: Environment variables to set for the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.auto_snapshot_args_resolved_modules
documentation:
If enabled, `nshsnap` will automatically look through the function
arguments and snapshot any third-party modules that are resolved.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._runner.Config.auto_snapshot_editable
documentation: If enabled, `nshsnap` will automatically snapshot any editable packages.

functions.name: nshrunner._util.environment.remove_slurm_environment_variables
documentation:
SLURM_CPU_BIND_* environment variables are set by SLURM in the current environment.
We need to remove all of these environment variables during the codepath in which we create the new SLURM runs, so that the new SLURM runs do not inherit the environment variables from the current environment.
To make things easier, we will patch the environment to remove all "SLURM_" environment variables.
Otherwise, the runs will faill with an error like shown below:
    srun: error: CPU binding outside of job step allocation, allocated CPUs are: 0x01F000000001F0000000.
    srun: error: Task launch for StepId=5216715.0 failed on node learnfair0537: Unable to satisfy cpu bind request
    srun: error: Application launch failed: Unable to satisfy cpu bind request
    srun: Job step aborted

See https://www.mail-archive.com/slurm-users@lists.schedmd.com/msg09157.html for more details.

functions.name: nshrunner._util.environment.remove_lsf_environment_variables
documentation: Same as above, but for the LSF task scheduler.

functions.name: nshrunner._util.environment.remove_wandb_environment_variables
documentation: Similar to above, but removes all "WANDB_" environment variables.

functions.name: nshrunner._util.environment.remove_nshrunner_environment_variables
documentation: Similar to above, but removes all "NSHRUNNER_" environment variables.

functions.name: nshrunner._util.environment.set_additional_env_vars
documentation:
Set additional environment variables for the run.
Newly set environment variables will be removed after the run is finished.
Existing environment variables will be restored to their original values after the run is finished.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._seed.SeedConfig.seed
documentation: Seed for the random number generator.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._seed.SeedConfig.seed_workers
documentation: Whether to seed the workers of the dataloader (Only applicable to PyTorch Lightning).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._seed.SeedConfig.use_lightning
documentation: Whether to use Lightning's seed_everything function (if available).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.log_level
documentation: Log level to use for the Python logger.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.log_save_dir
documentation: Directory to save logs to. If None, logs will not be saved.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.treescope
documentation: If enabled, will use the treescope library to visualize data structures in notebooks.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.treescope_autovisualize_arrays
documentation: If enabled, will automatically visualize arrays with treescope (if `treescope` is enabled).

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.lovely_tensors
documentation: If enabled, will use the lovely-tensors library to format PyTorch tensors. False by default as it causes issues when used with `torch.vmap`.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.lovely_numpy
documentation: If enabled, will use the lovely-numpy library to format numpy arrays. False by default as it causes some issues with other libaries.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.rich
documentation: If enabled, will use the rich library to format the Python logger output.

class_variables.name: nshrunner._logging.PythonLoggingConfig.rich_tracebacks
documentation: If enabled, will use the rich library to format the Python logger tracebacks.

functions.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create._write_helper_script
documentation:
Creates a helper bash script for running the given function.

The core idea: The helper script is essentially one additional layer of indirection
that allows us to encapsulates the environment setup and the actual function call
in a single bash script (that does not require properly set up Python environment).

In effect, this allows us to, for example:
- Easily run the function in the correct environment
    (without having to deal with shell hooks)
    using `conda run -n myenv bash /path/to/helper.sh`.
- Easily run the function in a Singularity container
    using `singularity exec my_container.sif bash /path/to/helper.sh`.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.SequentialExecutionConfig.print_environment_info
documentation: Print the environment information before starting the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.SequentialExecutionConfig.python_executable
documentation: Python executable to use for running the script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.SequentialExecutionConfig.pause_before_exit
documentation: Wait for the user to press Enter after finishing.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.ArrayExecutionConfig.job_index_variable
documentation: Name of the environment variable that contains the job index. E.g., `SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID` for Slurm.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.ArrayExecutionConfig.print_environment_info
documentation: Print the environment information before starting the session.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.ArrayExecutionConfig.python_executable
documentation: Python executable to use for running the script.

class_variables.name: nshrunner.picklerunner.create.ArrayExecutionConfig.pause_before_exit
documentation: Wait for the user to press Enter after finishing.
